title: Connect product detail and guest cart UI
verify: A valid Medusa Product Variant ID from product detail reaches guest-cart add without weakening selection guards.
Cart page renders backend items/totals and supports absolute quantity update and remove.
Loading, empty, stale, validation, stock conflict, and backend failure states are visible and recoverable.
Reload renders the retrieved backend cart rather than cached line data.

verify.summary: Manual T2 functional verification passed. The required packet was ready and hash-matched before verification; linked FT-003 UX/API/runtime specs were read; fresh cart-view/product-detail tests, storefront typecheck, Memory Bank lint, strict doctor, and full storefront regression passed. Product detail sends the FT-002 valid Medusa Product Variant ID into guest-cart add; /cart renders backend cart truth, supports absolute update/remove, exposes loading/empty/stale/validation/stock-conflict/backend-failure states, and reload restores via backend reference without browser-authoritative cart data. Task status remains unchanged pending scheduler or explicit closure decision; FT-003 feature completion still requires feature-level /red-verify --feature FT-003.

verify.closure_decision: Manual T2 closure explicitly requested by the user after existing /verify PASS evidence.
verify.summary: GENERAL closed TASK-023 in manual mode after explicit user instruction. Required T2 gates are satisfied: full protocol evidence, required packet/spec gates, /verify PASS, and fresh closure checks pass. No per-task /red-verify or T3 markers are required for this T2 task. FT-003 remains planned because downstream TASK-026 and feature-level semantic verification are not closed.

evidence_required: Component evidence for success, empty, stale, conflict, and failure states.
Storefront typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Preserve FT-002 valid-variant handoff and blocked-selection behavior.
invariants: Only a valid Medusa Product Variant ID reaches add-to-cart.
UI state cannot change ownership or claim uncommitted success.
purpose: Turn guest-cart state orchestration into the buyer-visible add, view, update, remove, and reload flow.
success_outcome: A buyer can use a persistent backend-owned guest cart from product detail through the cart page.
anti_goals: Do not implement authenticated merge, login, checkout, order, or payment.
Do not replace FT-002 selection validation.
Do not add browser-authoritative cart data.

forbidden_scope: checkout, order, inventory reservation, or payment
stop_conditions: FT-002 no longer supplies a valid Product Variant ID.
Rendered success cannot be tied to a backend cart response.
Required UI behavior conflicts with the linked UX contract.
